items.title: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002)
items.why_it_matters: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

items.title: Headlines
items.summary: Commentary: Workforce Pell: Governance & opportunity State Higher Education Executive Officers Association (blog) State higher education agencies bring significant assets to Workforce Pell implementation, including convening authority, subject matter expertise and robust data and analytical... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: US Department of Labor issues guidance declaring proxy advisors may be investment advice fiduciaries
items.summary: WASHINGTON – The U.S. Department of Labor’s Employee Benefits Security Administration has issued first-of-its-kind guidance stating that proxy advisory firms commonly engage in business practices that meet the test for being investment advice fiduciaries. In a recently published Technical Release, the department spells out that proxy advisors regularly engage in conduct that makes them investment advice fiduciaries u

items.title: MassEducate
items.why_it_matters: Useful anchor for Massachusetts affordability policy and how access commitments interact with advising, persistence, and completion capacity.

items.title: MassReconnect
items.why_it_matters: Important Massachusetts reference point for adult student re-engagement, affordability, and the operational demands placed on community colleges.

items.title: Anna Maria College flagged as closure risk by state
items.summary: Massachusetts’ higher education department said it can’t confirm the institution “has sufficient resources to be able to sustain operations at current levels.”

items.title: Hampshire College to close after years-long turnaround effort comes up short
items.summary: The private institution in Massachusetts faced a heavy debt burden, persistent deficits, sputtering enrollment, possible accreditation loss and other challenges.

items.title: The college transfer generation
items.summary: For decades, students transferring between colleges was a side road in American higher education. Today, it’s one of the system’s main highways. The National Student Clearinghouse Research Center reports that nearly 1.2 million students, or... The post The college transfer generation first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: MassDHE News Clips: Community Colleges
items.why_it_matters: MassDHE’s community college clips often surface state-level transfer, workforce, affordability, and funding developments.

items.title: MassTransfer
items.why_it_matters: Anchor page for Massachusetts transfer policy, useful when comparing other states’ transfer, articulation, and pathway reforms.

items.title: ED’s plan to weed out fake student aid applications
items.summary: The U.S. Education Department (ED) this week detailed its timeline and implementation of real-time identity screening procedures for applicants completing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA). The changes aim to address the rise... The post ED’s plan to weed out fake student aid applications first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Shifting focus to post-completion outcomes
items.summary: SEATTLE — Over the last 15 years, colleges have shifted from a focus on access to one on student success — primarily credential completion. Now, colleges need to focus on a different success metric: post-completion... The post Shifting focus to post-completion outcomes first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Building AI Initiatives with Bottom-Up Champions
items.summary: Driven by a bottom-up partnership between the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning and the Division of Digital Learning, the University of Central Florida established an evolving campus infrastructure of policies, training, and a national conference to guide the ethical and effective integration of generative AI into teaching and learning.

items.title: Headlines
items.summary: Students earning credentials at younger ages Insider Higher Ed More than 3.4 million people earned an undergraduate credential during the 2024–25 academic year, a 3.2% increase over 2023–24, according to a new report from the... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Virtual Fellowships a ‘Lifeline’ for Gazan Academics
items.summary: Virtual Fellowships a ‘Lifeline’ for Gazan Academics Susan H. Greenberg Fri, 04/17/2026 - 03:00 AM Universities find ways to help scholars keep work going despite widespread destruction of higher ed institutions and research facilities. Byline(s) Juliette Rowsell for Times Higher Education

items.title: ‘There is no silver bullet’: How 2 colleges use AI to support nontraditional learners
items.summary: Artificial intelligence can help bridge gaps for students, but it isn't as simple as buying a bunch of products, college leaders said at the ASU+GSV Summit.

items.title: Walla Walla Community College board votes to close satellite campus
items.summary: Even as they prepare to shutter the Clarkston, Washington, location, officials are exploring alternative funding to keep a physical presence in the area.

items.title: Kentucky lawmakers override veto on bill easing faculty terminations
items.summary: Under the legislation, public colleges will be able to let go of professors for “bona fide financial reasons,” including if programs have low enrollment.

items.title: Syracuse University offers early retirement to 175 faculty
items.summary: The buyouts come amid a plan to cut scores of low-enrollment academic programs to refocus the private nonprofit’s offerings.

items.title: What Makes Edtech Work for Students [Infographic]
items.summary: Even the most well-intentioned edtech can fall short if it does not meet students where they are. After several years studying the usability of edtech ...

items.title: The Human Side of Data at Any Scale
items.summary: Sophie and Jenay talk with Nadeem Syed of Western Governor's University and Shannon Shank of California Institute of the Arts about how institutions of any size can consider foundational approaches to data for informed decision-making.

items.title: Hampshire College Will Close Amid Student Enrollment Declines
items.summary: Other small private colleges like Hampshire have closed in recent years as financial pressures and competition for students increase.

items.title: University of Michigan’s incoming president withdraws after cancer diagnosis
items.summary: Kent Syverud, who led Syracuse University for over a decade, would have begun in July. Now, the public institution is restarting its search for a leader.

items.title: Sonny Perdue to retire as University System of Georgia leader
items.summary: The chancellor has led the system for four years and will stay on until its board selects his successor, per a Wednesday statement.

items.title: AI is remaking the workforce. How can colleges ensure students thrive?
items.summary: Skills training and public-private partnerships are key in a rapidly changing job landscape, higher ed leaders said this week at the annual ASU+GSV Summit.

items.title: Mounting Troubles at Ohio State University Lead to Frustration
items.summary: Ohio State isn’t the only university in turmoil, but few others have faced so many issues lately. One lawmaker called the school “a national embarrassment.”

items.title: Texas A&M Picks an Insider for President After Months of Conflict
items.summary: Debates over how to teach about gender, sexuality and other topics have shaken the school, and led to the ouster of the previous president at the College Station campus last summer.

items.title: After Charlie Kirk’s Death, a Fight for the Youth Vote
items.summary: A fledgling Democratic group and the far-right white nationalist Nick Fuentes seek to make gains on college campuses that have recently been dominated by Turning Point USA.

items.title: Newsmakers
items.summary: New CEOs David M. Knezek, Jr., has been appointed as the seventh president of Henry Ford College (Michigan). He is currently chief operating officer of the Michigan Department of Health and Human Services and an... The post Newsmakers first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: AACC’s Strategic Compass 2035 as a living document
items.summary: SEATTLE — The new president and CEO of the American Association of Community Colleges (AACC) this past weekend unveiled her vision for the association’s work for the next decade, but DeRionne Pollard is clear that... The post AACC’s Strategic Compass 2035 as a living document first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Snapshots: Final pics from AACC 2026 in Seattle
items.summary: A few more photos to wrap up the American Association of Community Colleges annual convention in Seattle. See you next year in Indianapolis! The post Snapshots: Final pics from AACC 2026 in Seattle first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Headlines
items.summary: Gov. Abbott touts Texas Jobs Council to strengthen skilled workforce Lubbock Avalanche-Journal “There are approximately 2.6 million Texans with some college credits but no credentials,” said Texas Association of Community Colleges President and CEO Ray... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Climate Knowledge Linked to Student Stress
items.summary: Climate Knowledge Linked to Student Stress Joshua.Bay Fri, 04/17/2026 - 03:00 AM A new study from Quinnipiac University finds that greater awareness of climate change affects students’ mental health—but also drives them to rethink habits and career paths. Byline(s) Joshua Bay

items.title: Yale President: University Helped Erode Public Trust in Higher Ed
items.summary: Yale President: University Helped Erode Public Trust in Higher Ed Ryan Quinn Fri, 04/17/2026 - 03:00 AM Byline(s) Ryan Quinn

items.title: Award winners display the transformative nature of community colleges
items.summary: SEATTLE — Ten awards were given out Monday night at the American Association of Community Colleges‘ annual Awards of Excellence gala, in categories ranging from advancing institutional equity and belonging, to CEO of the year.... The post Award winners display the transformative nature of community colleges first appeared on Community College Daily .

items.title: Donations to colleges hold steady amid higher ed disruption
items.summary: The growth reflects the “continued trust that donors place in educational institutions,” the Council for Advancement and Support of Education said.

items.title: To Fill Drone Force Ranks, Russia Targets a New Demographic: Students
items.summary: Recruitment sessions have taken place at hundreds of universities and colleges across the country.

items.title: In a Restaurant’s Name, a Message of Grief and Hope for Palestinians
items.summary: Hinds Hall near Columbia University is a place to find Palestinian cuisine and to remember a 5-year-old killed in Gaza.

items.title: Cookie House at Carleton College in Minnesota Keeps Students and Others Baking
items.summary: For nearly 80 years, Carleton College in Minnesota has kept a place where students and others can come, bake and share. After a recent renovation, we checked in.

top_signals.title: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.
top_signals.summary_for_brief: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.
top_signals.core_story: The core story here is about whether public policy is backing institutional expectations with real dollars and implementation capacity.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: This can be framed as an investment comparison: what level of funding is another state prepared to put behind reform, and how does that compare with current commitments in Massachusetts?
top_signals.recommended_angle: Frame this as an investment-gap question for Massachusetts: are leaders expecting community colleges to do system-changing work without system-level funding?
top_signals.claim: Massachusetts is increasingly treating advising, transfer support, and wraparound services as core completion infrastructure rather than optional supports.
top_signals.implication: The key question is no longer whether these supports matter, but whether colleges and the state can fund and scale them effectively.

top_signals.title: Headlines
top_signals.summary: Commentary: Workforce Pell: Governance & opportunity State Higher Education Executive Officers Association (blog) State higher education agencies bring significant assets to Workforce Pell implementation, including convening authority, subject matter expertise and robust data and analytical... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .
top_signals.why_it_matters: Could affect aid eligibility, workforce pathways, grants, reporting, or institutional compliance.
top_signals.summary_for_brief: Commentary: Workforce Pell: Governance & opportunity State Higher Education Executive Officers Association (blog) State higher education agencies bring significant assets to Workforce Pell implementation, including convening authority, subject matter expertise and robust data and analytical... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .
top_signals.core_story: The core story is how policy shifts translate into operational consequences for colleges and students.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: This story may offer a useful comparison point for policy and practice in Massachusetts, especially if leaders are asking whether ambition is matched by implementation capacity.
top_signals.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.
top_signals.claim: Federal policy is accelerating the shift toward short-term, workforce-aligned programs as a central community college strategy.
top_signals.implication: Community colleges will need stronger advising, employer alignment, and outcomes reporting if they want to benefit from the next phase of federal support.

top_signals.summary: For decades, students transferring between colleges was a side road in American higher education. Today, it’s one of the system’s main highways. The National Student Clearinghouse Research Center reports that nearly 1.2 million students, or... The post The college transfer generation first appeared on Community College Daily .
top_signals.why_it_matters: Relevant to advising practice, student persistence, retention strategy, and transfer support.

The National Student Clearinghouse Research Center reports that nearly 1.2 million students, or... The post The college transfer generation first appeared on Community College Daily .
top_signals.core_story: The core story is whether advising is being treated as institutional infrastructure rather than a reactive student-services function.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: This story may offer a useful comparison point for policy and practice in Massachusetts, especially if leaders are asking whether ambition is matched by implementation capacity.
top_signals.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.
top_signals.claim: Advising is emerging as a structural lever for completion, transfer, and workforce success—not just a student-services function.
top_signals.implication: Colleges that treat advising as institutional infrastructure will be better positioned than those relying on fragmented or purely reactive models.

top_signals.title: Building AI Initiatives with Bottom-Up Champions
top_signals.summary: Driven by a bottom-up partnership between the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning and the Division of Digital Learning, the University of Central Florida established an evolving campus infrastructure of policies, training, and a national conference to guide the ethical and effective integration of generative AI into teaching and learning.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Relevant to AI governance, staff development, classroom use, or student-support operations.
top_signals.summary_for_brief: Driven by a bottom-up partnership between the Faculty Center for Teaching and Learning and the Division of Digital Learning, the University of Central Florida established an evolving campus infrastructure of policies, training, and a national conference to guide the ethical and effective integration of generative AI into teaching and learning.
top_signals.core_story: The deeper issue is whether institutions are moving from AI curiosity to real implementation supported by governance, staffing, and strategy.
top_signals.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: For Massachusetts, the more important question is not whether AI tools exist, but whether there is meaningful investment in governed, student-centered implementation.
top_signals.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.
top_signals.claim: AI adoption in higher education is outpacing institutional clarity about governance, training, and appropriate use.
top_signals.implication: The institutions that benefit most will be the ones that define clear use cases and guardrails before AI becomes embedded everywhere.

briefing_notes.core_story: The deeper issue is whether institutions are moving from AI curiosity to real implementation supported by governance, staffing, and strategy.
briefing_notes.state_relevance: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them.
comparison_points: For Massachusetts, the more important question is not whether AI tools exist, but whether there is meaningful investment in governed, student-centered implementation.
briefing_notes.recommended_angle: Use this as a policy comparison story that helps leaders in Massachusetts think about investment, implementation, and institutional capacity.

linkedin_drafts.title: Massachusetts policy watch: a comparison worth making
linkedin_drafts.text:
One higher-ed development worth watching this week is MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/).

Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

What stands out to me is that The core story here is about whether public policy is backing institutional expectations with real dollars and implementation capacity.

For Massachusetts, the key question is whether community colleges are being given the investment, staffing, and policy infrastructure needed to do similar work at scale.

Why this matters: For Massachusetts, the value of this story is comparative. It raises the question of whether community colleges are being funded and supported at a level that matches the expectations being placed on them. Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

That is where the real policy conversation should begin.

#Massachusetts #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #HigherEdPolicy #AcademicAdvising

linkedin_drafts.title: Massachusetts student success infrastructure
linkedin_drafts.text:
Massachusetts is increasingly treating advising, transfer support, and wraparound services as core completion infrastructure rather than optional supports.

A good example is MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002) (https://budget.digital.mass.gov/govbudget/fy27/appropriations/education/higher-education/71004002/). Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language, funding, and wraparound-support intent through the budget process.

What matters for community colleges is this: The key question is no longer whether these supports matter, but whether colleges and the state can fund and scale them effectively.

That is where the real strategic question sits right now—not just what changed, but how institutions respond.

#Massachusetts #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #HigherEdPolicy

linkedin_drafts.title: Workforce Pell and short-term pathways
linkedin_drafts.text:
Federal policy is accelerating the shift toward short-term, workforce-aligned programs as a central community college strategy.

A good example is Headlines (https://www.ccdaily.com/2026/04/headlines-1157/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=headlines-1157). Commentary: Workforce Pell: Governance & opportunity State Higher Education Executive Officers Association (blog) State higher education agencies bring significant assets to Workforce Pell implementation, including convening authority, subject matter expertise and robust data and analytical... The post Headlines first appeared on Community College Daily .

What matters for community colleges is this: Community colleges will need stronger advising, employer alignment, and outcomes reporting if they want to benefit from the next phase of federal support.

That is where the real strategic question sits right now—not just what changed, but how institutions respond.

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #EdTech

linkedin_drafts.title: Advising and student support
linkedin_drafts.text:
Advising is emerging as a structural lever for completion, transfer, and workforce success—not just a student-services function.

A good example is The college transfer generation (https://www.ccdaily.com/2026/04/the-college-transfer-generation/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=the-college-transfer-generation). For decades, students transferring between colleges was a side road in American higher education. Today, it’s one of the system’s main highways. The National Student Clearinghouse Research Center reports that nearly 1.2 million students, or... The post The college transfer generation first appeared on Community College Daily .

What matters for community colleges is this: Colleges that treat advising as institutional infrastructure will be better positioned than those relying on fragmented or purely reactive models.

That is where the real strategic question sits right now—not just what changed, but how institutions respond.

#HigherEdPolicy #CommunityColleges #StudentSuccess #AcademicAdvising #EdTech
